codeReview.summary: The feature delivers substantial approval-workflow functionality, but it misses a required submission path and contains a server-side authorization gap for approver actions.

issues.description: The Add Employee / EmployeeDetail flow still creates employees directly (`addEmployee`) instead of submitting a headcount request into the approval pipeline. The only wired request-entry point is the toolbar dialog (`Request Hire` in Toolbar/AppShell), so the requirement to submit from EmployeeDetailPanel or Add Employee flow is not met.

`ensureActionable` authorizes actions only by request status + current-step approver ID and never verifies org membership. Since chain creation/update only validates approver IDs as ObjectId format (not org membership) in `approvalChainController`, a non-member user can be configured as approver and successfully call approve/reject/bulk action endpoints directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Authorization conventions are ambiguous for approval action endpoints: architecture docs emphasize role/membership middleware, but these routes rely on controller-level approver checks without org-membership enforcement.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md states role-based org membership middleware; packages/server/src/routes/approvalRoutes.ts:91-100 mounts action endpoints with only `auth`; packages/server/src/controllers/headcountRequestController.ts:402-450 lacks org membership check.

summary: Fail. Key blockers: (1) Add Employee / EmployeeDetail flow bypasses approvals instead of submitting requests, and (2) approver-action authorization does not enforce org membership, enabling non-member approver IDs to act via direct API calls.
